name: Lasagna Bolognese
tagline: Rich beef-pork ragù layered with pasta, béchamel, parmesan

hero.alt: Slice of layered lasagna with ragù and béchamel
hero.caption: lasagna bolognese — layered slow ragù

steps.title: Slow-build the ragù
steps.detail: Render pancetta. Add soffritto, cook 10 minutes. Add beef and pork, brown deeply. Stir in tomato paste; cook 3 minutes. Pour in 1 cup milk; reduce. Add stock and simmer 90 minutes.
steps.tip: Milk first, then tomato — milk tenderizes the meat before acid would tighten it.

steps.title: Whisk the béchamel
steps.detail: Melt butter, whisk in flour to form a roux. Cook 2 minutes. Slowly whisk in 3 cups warm milk; cook until thick. Season with salt and a tiny grate of nutmeg.
steps.tip: Warm milk into a hot roux — cold milk equals lumps.

steps.title: Layer six high
steps.detail: Pasta, ragù, béchamel, parmesan — repeat six times. Finish with béchamel and a heavy parmesan layer for the crust.
steps.tip: Boil fresh pasta sheets 30 seconds before layering; dried no-boil are fine but soak up more sauce.

steps.title: Bake and rest
steps.detail: Bake at 375°F for 40 minutes covered, then 15 uncovered for color. Rest 20 minutes before cutting — the layers need to set.
steps.tip: Lasagna eaten the next day is even better. Letting it set overnight in the fridge tightens the layers.

healthFacts: A real bolognese has very little tomato — it's mostly meat, milk, and stock.
Béchamel uses fat-soluble nutmeg traditionally; even a hint adds depth.
